author: m.kalinin
content: i don't fully get (1)

author: m.kalinin
content:
> the parent beacon block is not passed to the  EL since this block is synced over devp2p, but the value used is committed in the block hash. 

And we don't check that `parent_hash` commits to the same content on CL and EL, EL checks `parent_hash` when processing parent payload with its own values in the block header, CL doesn't check anything except for accumulator which lacks `parent_beacon_block_root`. This is the place whether the thing gets broken

author: potuz
content: Yeah it's fine to add the parent root.. But also notice that we will add that root to the Payload anyway so it'll be easier to track on both sides. When that PR merges I'll update the EIP

author: m.kalinin
content: You mean adding that root to the `ExecutionPayload` container?

author: potuz
content: Yes

author: m.kalinin
content: there was a recent PR by Etan to add that field to the Envelope, but we could directly add it to the payload with the same effect

author: potuz
content: That's the one I'm talking about

author: potuz
content: We agreed to merge that

author: potuz
content: So when that merges I'll update this EIP

author: m.kalinin
content: it's merged https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5152

author: potuz
content: Oh right!

author: m.kalinin
content: but it's not in the Payload, it's in the Envelope, while I think it could be in the Payload with the same outcome

author: m.kalinin
content: and we could remove `parent_beacon_block_root` from the `newPayload` params as it's already in the `ExecutionPayload`

author: nero_eth
content: For those not at interop following along. We decided against 8237, so, for gloas we will have to keep downloading payloads on both layers, CL (=payload), and EL (bodies)

author: taulepton_
content: Thanks for the update 🙏 what was the justification for dropping it? Will this be dropped entirely or delayed to a future hard fork?

author: nero_eth
content:
It would have come with some complexity for some clients and might be reconsidered in the future (could even be reconsidered for glamsterdam) but the decision was agaist putting it on glamsterdam-devnet-1 afaict.
Clients also want to rethink this topic from first priciple and come up with a better approach, e.g. CLs having RLP and checking the blocks directly

author: taulepton_
content: <@792404665068158998> makes sense. FYI some clients already have rlp and do these checks directly - see the message I linked (but potuz said for some range of blocks they don't do it - I'm unsure about this).

author: m.kalinin
content: the problem here is with duplication of the p2p traffic during the range sync, EL and CL pull the same payloads independently

author: taulepton_
content: yeah seems wasteful

author: m.kalinin
content: in general, not against additional considerations around this EIP

author: taulepton_
content: I think it would be good to consider EIP8025 / execution proofs when designing this EIP to ensure that the desired security properties are also upheld for execution proof based payload validity.

author: m.kalinin
content: btw, shall we still move `parent_beacon_block_root` from Envelope to Payload?

content: <@881905303011086387> what do you think on this?
